content.claim: In Hungary, socioeconomic status predicts access to early-selective academic tracks independently of prior test scores, and this sorting is stronger than in other OECD countries.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Education finance lens
limitations: Exact magnitude of independent SES effect not quantified in notes
Value-added estimates for grades 6–8 and 8–10 cited but specific coefficients not provided
Does not isolate causal effect of 6/8-year track design versus general school-choice mechanism
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
